90558.details: Teaches the basic concepts of the thermodynamic processes in the atmosphere and their applications. Starting from the composition and structure of  the atmosphere to the laws of thermodynamics, introduces stability and cloud-related processes. Employs atmospheric datasets for analysis and visualization. Cross-listed with MET 5501 Advanced Atmospheric  Thermodynamics. (Prerequisites: PHY 2002)
90558.Title: Atmospheric Thermodynamics

90559.details: Teaches the basic concepts of the thermodynamic processes in the atmosphere and their applications. Starting from the composition and structure of the atmosphere to the laws of thermodynamics,  introduces stability and cloud-related processes. Employs atmospheric datasets for analysis and visualization. Cross-listed with MET 4501 Atmospheric Thermodynamics. Requires background knowledge equivalent to PHY 2002.
90559.Title: Adv Atmos Thermo

90560.details: Provides a supplement to  OCN 1010, Introduction to Oceanography. Students gain practical experience in the methodologies, sampling and analyses used by oceanographers through field trips and laboratory experiments, Focuses on how to collect and interpret data and begin to prepare reports. (Prerequisites: OCN 1010, OCN 1010)
90560.Title: Intro Ocean Lab

90561.details: Special topics not covered in the regular curriculum, offered to specific student groups. May be repeated for a maximum of nine credits. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90561.Title: Spc Top in Oceanography
90561.Notes: TOPIC: Physical Oceanography of Estuaries & Coasts

90562.details: Special topics not covered in the regular curriculum. Offered on occasion to specific student groups. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90562.Title: Spc Top in Oceanography
90562.Notes: TOPIC: Physical Oceanography of Estuaries & Coasts

90563.details: Special course topics not covered in the regular curriculum, offered on occasion to specific student groups. May be repeated for a maximum of nine credits. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90563.Title: Spc Top in Enviro Sci

90564.details: Special course topics not covered in the regular curriculum. Offered on occasion to specific student groups. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90564.Title: Special Topics in ENS

90565.details: Reading and discussions of recent advances and new concepts in ecological research.
90565.Title: Current Topics in Ecology

90566.details: Introduces techniques to study the cardiovascular system as an aid in designing treatments and diagnosing health conditions. Includes cardiovascular  anatomy and physiology, system modeling and assist devices, cardiac bioelectricity and mechanisms of arrhythmias, hemodynamics and pulsatile blood flow, and other topics related to heart mechanics. Note: Undergraduate students from other disciplines may take this course with the instructor's approval. (Prerequisites: BME 3240, BME 3240)
90566.Title: Cardiovascular Engineering

90569.details: Discusses the instrumentation and analytics for noninvasive brain imaging and neural sensing. Topics include time and and frequency domain analytics, statistics and experimental design. (Prerequisites: BME 4444)
90569.Title: Neuroengineering 2

90570.details: Discusses the instrumentation and analytics for noninvasive brain imaging and neural sensing. Topics include time and frequency domain analytics, statistics and experimental design. (Prerequisites: BME 4444, BME 5444)
90570.Title: Neuroengineering 2

90571.details: Covers special topics in biomedical engineering. May be repeated for credit. (Requirement: Junior standing)
90571.Title: Special Topics in BME

90572.details: Provides an overview of the space environment during spaceflight, discusses the physiological changes associated with astronauts adapting to microgravity and space radiation environment, evaluates the countermeasures for mitigation of spaceflight associated health hazards, and imparts how human factors are used in space related design.
90572.Title: Space Based Biomed Eng App

90573.details: Introduces medical imaging technologies from a biomedical engineering perspective. Discusses instrumentation, physics, mathematics and clinical applications of medical imaging modalities including x-ray, computed tomography, magnetic resonance imaging, positron emission tomography, ultrasound, optical, fluorescence and molecular imaging. (Prerequisites: BIO 3201, MTH 2201, MTH 3200, PHY 2002)
90573.Title: Intro Biomedical Imaging

90574.details: Presents the interdisciplinary principles of medical imaging techniques such as diagnostic ultrasound, radiography, x-ray computer tomography (CT) and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). Includes the physical principles, noise modeling and signal processing for each imaging modality. (Prerequisites: MTH 2201, MTH 3200, MTH 2401)
90574.Title: Medical Imaging

90575.details: Studies a particular genre of music, composer or historical period. Focuses on analytical techniques and cultural contexts. Topic announced before registration. (HU) (LA) (Prerequisites: HUM 2051, HUM 2052, HUM 2053, HUM 2054, HUM 2141, HUM 2142, HUM 2211, HUM 2212, HUM 2213, HUM 2331, HUM 2332, HUM 2551, HUM 2552)
90575.Title: Spc Top in Music Lit

90576.details: Provides instruction to students on the writing process with a focus on grammar and syntax. Reviews grammar, syntax, spelling and punctuation rules, and developing paragraphs into essays to prepare for higher-level courses. Required for students with placement test scores below 30. Credit cannot be applied toward any Florida Tech degree.
90576.Title: College English Grammar

90578.details: Examines experimental evidence on the physical, physiological and psychological effects of drug use and conclusions relating to the real vs. alleged effects of drugs. (LA) (SS)
90578.Title: Substance Abuse

90580.details: Offers research experience under the direction of a member of the psychology faculty, generally in the context of programmatic research teams. May be repeated for a maximum of three credits. (LA) (Prerequisites: PSY 1411)
90580.Title: Research Experience

90581.details: Offers research experience under the direction of a member of the psychology faculty. May be repeated for a maximum of six credits. (LA) (Requirement: Instructor approval.) (Prerequisites: PSY 3513, PSY 4514)
90581.Title: Undergraduate Research

90582.details: Offers honor's students advanced research experience under the discretion of a member of the psychology faculty. Students are required to have a substantive deliverable based on their research by the end of the semester. May be repeated for a maximum of six credits. (Prerequisites: PSY 4590)
90582.Title: Psychology Honors Research

90583.details: Includes the preparation of an undergraduate thesis under supervision of a faculty member. Involves all components of the research process, including conceptualization, literature review, method and hypothesis development, data collection and analysis, and preparation of the final document. May be repeated for a total of six credits. (Requirement: Acceptance to psychology honors program.) (LA) (Q) (Prerequisites: PSY 3513, PSY 4514)
90583.Title: Psychology Honors Thesis

90584.details: Offers honor's students advanced research experience under the discretion of a member of the psychology faculty. Students are required to have a substantive deliverable based on their research by the end of the semester. May be repeated for a maximum of six credits. (Prerequisites: PSY 4590)
90584.Title: Psychology Honors Research

90585.details: Includes the preparation of an undergraduate thesis under supervision of a faculty member. Involves all components of the research process, including conceptualization, literature review, method and hypothesis development, data collection and analysis, and preparation of the final document. May be repeated for a total of six credits. (Requirement: Acceptance to psychology honors program.) (LA) (Q) (Prerequisites: PSY 3513, PSY 4514)
90585.Title: Psychology Honors Thesis

90586.details: Offers research experience under the direction of a member of the psychology faculty, generally in the context of programmatic research teams. May be repeated for a maximum of three credits. (LA) (Prerequisites: PSY 1411)
90586.Title: Research Experience

90587.details: Offers research experience under the direction of a member of the psychology faculty. May be repeated for a maximum of six credits. (LA) (Requirement: Instructor approval.) (Prerequisites: PSY 3513, PSY 4514)
90587.Title: Undergraduate Research

90588.details: Covers the scientific principles, theories and research about learning in both humans and other animals. Examines the learning process and how organisms acquire, modify and retain behaviors over time through an evolutionary perspective. Topics include classical and operant conditioning, observational learning, and cognitive and social influences on learning. (Prerequisites: PSY 2512, MAR 2801)
90588.Title: Learning

90589.details: Covers the many ways psychology is applied in organizations to improve performance and quality of work life. Includes employee selection and personnel law, performance management, training, motivation, job attitudes, stress, teamwork, leadership and organizational development. (LA) (SS) (Prerequisites: BUS 2703, CRM 3012, EST 2703, PSY 2512, PSY 3012)
90589.Title: Psychology of Workplace

90591.details: Surveys major interventions associated with group and team development within organizations. Interventions include group and team assessment, creative problem solving, decision making, resolving conflicts and management by objectives.
90591.Title: Group & Team Development

90592.details: Introduces the guiding principles of brain-behavior interactions derived from the basic disciplines of psychology, neuroanatomy and neuropathology. Provides the scientific basis for neuropsychological and cognitive assessment. (Prerequisites: PSY 5105)
90592.Title: Fund of Clin Neuropsych

90593.details: Explores theories of leadership from an interactional perspective that includes the leader, followers and the context. Covers leadership theories, leadership development, team leadership, ethics, executive-level leadership, and power and influence.
90593.Title: Leadership Seminar

90594.details: Studies the nature, etiology, characteristics, assessment and treatment of emotional, social and intellectual problems of children.
90594.Title: Child Disorders

90595.details: Covers the fundamentals of computer vision, a subfield of artificial intelligence, and computer science that focuses on how computers can be made to gain understanding from digital images or videos. Topics include image processing, feature extraction, object recognition, 3D vision and machine learning techniques for computer vision tasks. (Prerequisites: CSE 2010, MTH 1001, MTH 1002, MTH 3102, MTH 2401, CSE 2050)
90595.Title: Intro to Computer Vision

90596.details: Focuses on advanced skills required in software engineering to handle the complexities of machine learning-based software. Covers the different phases in machine learning pipeline such as data engineering, feature engineering, testing, training, deployment, continuous monitoring for model assessment and improvement. Additionally, addresses the differences from traditional software engineering. (Prerequisites: CSE 4301, MTH 4224)
90596.Title: Machine Learning Engineering

90597.details: Introduces students to the main AI problems in robotics: planning, localization, mapping and communication. Covers relevant concepts related to robots and their components, as well as important robot types and sample robots. Introduces software architecture supporting artificial intelligence for robots. Introduces robot communication, development and testing software frameworks. Introduces sample AI algorithms for some of the robotic problems. (Prerequisites: CSE 4301)
90597.Title: Intro to AI for Robotics

90598.details: Describes the formation and evolution of planetary systems including our own solar system and exoplanetary systems. Includes using astronomical observations, cosmochemistry and physics to describe the process of planet formation from the collapse of protostellar clouds through to observed systems. (Prerequisites: CHM 1102, PHY 3011, PHY 3011, PHY 3060, PHY 3060)
90598.Title: Phy & Chm of Planet Form

90599.details: Introduces basic interior structural equations, energy generation processes, opacity, energy transport, radiation transport in stellar atmospheres, star formation, late stages of stellar evolution, stellar binaries and clusters. Special emphasis on analytic and numerical models relevant to the sun. (Requirement: Background knowledge equivalent to PHY 3011 and PHY 3060.)
90599.Title: Astrophysics 1

90600.details: Mechanical and thermal processes governing the interior structure and surfaces of the major and minor planetary bodies of the solar system. Includes the planetary crust, mantle, core, core-mantle interface, seismicity, density and elastic constants. (Requirement: Background knowledge equivalent to PHY 3011 and SPS 3010.)
90600.Title: Planetary Sci 1: Interiors

90603.details: Applies business management concepts and techniques to the decision-making and problem-solving processes and situations in an aviation business. Uses operations research techniques, process analysis, forecasting, and computer and mathematical modeling as tools. (Q) (Prerequisites: AVM 4501)
90603.Title: Avt Business Simulation

90604.details: Covers conceptual and operational avionics systems in air-transport aircraft. Includes communications, navigation, flight control, flight management and engine instrumentation systems, and various electronic displays. Focuses on the pilot's perspective for effective use of the entire suite of avionics in improved decision making and safety.
90604.Title: Complex Avt Systems

90605.details: Reviews models, processes and research in information processing; attention, short- and long-term memory, memory codes, visualization and imagery; forgetting, semantic organization, problem solving, decision-making, language, multilingualism, music cognition and cognitive development. (LA) (Prerequisites: PSY 2512, PSY 3012)
90605.Title: Human Cognition

90606.details: Introduces programming in an object-oriented language. Includes data structures. Aims to turn students with little or no programming experience into comfortable programmers. Also includes algorithms for use with stacks, queues and lists. Required for CIS majors.
90606.Title: Data Structures & Program

90607.details: Acts as program capstone course that entails design and implementation of a significant project within the purview of information systems. Requires students to propose a project and have it approved by the instructor. Noncredit for computer science or software engineering majors. Required for CIS majors. (Requirement: Academic unit head or program chair approval.)
90607.Title: Project in CIS

90609.details: Includes research skills and related competencies involved in the planning, conducting and reporting of applied research studies of the type required for a graduate degree.
90609.Title: Essentials of Ed Research

90610.details: Introduces and overviews the field of environmental education. Includes an overview of the history and definition of EE, models of environmental literacy and behavior, and published needs assessments and status reports. Concludes with an analysis of current needs/problems and opportunities in Florida.
90610.Title: Foundations of Enviro Ed

90611.details: Creation of a pilot study in preparation for the doctoral dissertation. (Requirement: Must be taken in the same semester in which comprehensive examinations are administered.)
90611.Title: Research Practicum

90612.details: Covers systematic measurement and analysis of data to improve decision accuracy. Key topics encompass statistical techniques used to understand how systems behave and optimize their performance, including hypothesis testing; data collection issues such as survey data, reliability, accuracy of measured data; data measurement tools and techniques; statistical process control; design of experiment methods; full and fractional designs and multiple regression analysis. Recommended: SYS 5350 and background knowledge equivalent to MTH 2401
90612.Title: Statistical Research Methods
90612.Notes: For NGLU students in Jan 2025 cohort

90613.details: Covers systematic measurement and analysis of data to improve decision accuracy. Key topics encompass statistical techniques used to understand how systems behave and optimize their performance, including hypothesis testing; data collection issues such as survey data, reliability, accuracy of measured data; data measurement tools and techniques; statistical process control; design of experiment methods; full and fractional designs and multiple regression analysis. Recommended: SYS 5350 and background knowledge equivalent to MTH 2401
90613.Title: Statistical Research Methods
90613.Notes: For NGLU Systems Eng students in Jan 2026 cohort

90614.details: Includes tools and techniques used in estimating cost of all phases of a system. Covers total system cost including research and development, investment and operation. Also includes the system life cycle (SLC) cost estimation process, SLC cost estimation models including discounted cash-flow analysis, activity-based costing, and cost-benefit calculations. Teaches cost scenario sensitivity analysis and design-to-cost concepts.
90614.Title: Sys Life Cycle Cost Est
90614.Notes: For NGLU students in Jan 2025 cohort

90615.details: Provides an in-depth study of systems requirements processes and tools. Includes concepts such as capturing stakeholder requirements, the importance of the concept of operations and the system development life-cycle process. (Requirement: Background knowledge equivalent to SYS 5310 Systems Engineering Principles)
90615.Title: Systems Requirements
90615.Notes: For NGLU Systems Eng students in Jan 2026 cohort

90618.details: Individual research work under the direction of a member of the graduate faculty on a selected topic. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90618.Title: Thesis

90619.details: Introduces the principles in systems engineering (SE) that deal with system life cycle phases. Emphasizes requirements and design methodologies. Includes SE definition; life cycle methodologies, tools and techniques; evaluation of system and technology alternatives; reliability and maintainability; trade-off models; and management tools and techniques. (Requirements: Junior standing and instructor approval.) (Prerequisites: MTH 2201, MTH 3200)
90619.Title: Sys Eng Principles
90619.Notes: Course will run for the Fall-1 term (first 8-weeks)

90620.details: Introduces the fundamental principles in systems engineering (SE) that deal with system life cycle phases with emphasis on requirement and design methodologies. Key topics include SE definition; life cycle methodologies, tools and techniques; evaluation of system and technology alternatives; reliability and maintainability; trade-off models; and SE management tools and techniques. Covers topics within the current version of the International Council on Systems Engineering (INCOSE) Systems Engineering Handbook. (Required: Background knowledge equivalent to MTH 2401: Probability and Statistics)
90620.Title: Systems Eng Principles
90620.Notes: Course will run for the Fall-1 term (first 8-weeks)

90621.details: Introduces discrete-event simulation for systems analysis and decision-making, an approach widely applicable across various disciplines  including systems engineering and other engineering fields, operations research and management science. Covers the simulation modeling process to develop conceptual and computational models, fit data to statistical distributions  and conduct model verification and validation activities. Also presents techniques for output analysis and statistical methods for evaluating alternatives. (Required: Background knowledge equivalent to MTH 2401 Probability and Statistics)
90621.Title: Sys Modeling & Analysis
90621.Notes: Course will run for the Fall-1 term (first 8-weeks)

90622.details: Provides an in-depth study of systems requirements processes and tools. Includes concepts such as capturing stakeholder requirements, the importance of the concept of operations and the system development life-cycle process. (Requirements: Junior standing and instructor approval.) (Prerequisites: MTH 2201, MTH 3200)
90622.Title: Systems Req Analysis
90622.Notes: Course will run for the Fall-2 term (second 8-weeks)

90623.details: Provides an in-depth study of systems requirements processes and tools. Includes concepts such as capturing stakeholder requirements, the importance of the concept of operations and the system development life-cycle process. (Requirement: Background knowledge equivalent to SYS 5310 Systems Engineering Principles)
90623.Title: Systems Requirements
90623.Notes: Course will run for the Fall-2 term (second 8-weeks)

90624.details: Describes model-based systems engineering (MBSE) life-cycle processes for a system of interest. Introduces the process of creating and integrating system representations using the Object Management Group?, Inc. (OMG?) systems modeling language (SysML?). Covers systems analysis and other technical processes within an MBSE project. Outlines the process of planning, deploying and managing MBSE initiatives. (Requirement: Background knowledge equivalent to SYS 5310 Systems Engineering Principles and SYS 5350 Systems Modeling and Analysis)
90624.Title: Model-Based Systems Eng
90624.Notes: Course will run for the Fall-2 term (second 8-weeks)

90625.details: Covers conservation of energy in a deformable continuous medium; solution of time-dependent homogeneous heat conduction problems using separation of variables, Duhamel's method, Green's function, analytical approximate methods and finite-difference methods; phase-change problems; inverse problem; bio-heat transfer modeling and solution methods. (Requirement: Background knowledge of heat transfer.)
90625.Title: Conduction Heat Transfer

90626.details: Introduces the fundamental concepts and principles (e.g., thermodynamics, transport and kinetics) in electrochemical energy conversion and storage systems, such as electrolyzers, fuel cells, batteries and  supercapacitors.
90626.Title: Electrochem Energy Sys

90627.details: Introduces industry-standard programming methods and tools for technical computing and engineering task automation. Includes design automation using CAD programs, task automation using spreadsheets, engineering databases  and ethical considerations in automation and AI tools. (Prerequisites: CSE 1100, CSE 1502, CSE 1503)
90627.Title: App Comp for Mech Engr

90628.details: Reinforces the activities associated with MEE 4071 and MEE 4171. Investigates the physics of heat transfer (conduction, convection, radiation) through the use of modern experimental techniques. (Prerequisites: MAE 4171, MEE 4171)
90628.Title: Heat Transfer Laboratory

90629.details: Covers the design of basic machine elements. Emphasizes failure prevention. Includes screws, fasteners, connections, welded/brazed joints, springs, bearings, gears, clutches, brakes, couplings, flywheels, flexible mechanical elements and shafts. (Prerequisites: MAE 2024, MEE 2024, AEE 3083, MAE 3083)
90629.Title: Dsgn Machine Elements

90631.details: Introduces the history, basic skill techniques, rules, terminology and participation in team sports. Includes volleyball, soccer, softball, basketball, flag football, badminton and Ultimate Frisbee. Also focuses on the five components of health-related fitness.
90631.Title: Intro to Team Sports

90632.details: Research experience under the direction and supervision of a member of the biological sciences faculty. (Requirement: Instructor approval.) (Q)
90632.Title: Undergrad Research 1

90636.details: Research under the guidance of a faculty member of the biological sciences in a selected area of biology.
90636.Title: Biological Research

90637.details: Research and preparation for the master's thesis.
90637.Title: Thesis

90638.details: Research and preparation for the doctoral dissertation. (Requirement: Admission to candidacy for the doctoral degree.)
90638.Title: Dissertation in Biological Sci

90639.details: Discusses the relationship of the main metabolic pathways and their importance to energy production. Provides deep understanding of the fundamental biochemistry that supports all living organisms.
90639.Title: Intermediate Metabolism

90640.details: The principles of protein biochemistry are studied in detail, with emphasis on three-dimensional structure, biological characteristics, and functional properties of biologically significant proteins. Protein structure and its relation to enzyme mechanisms and chemical catalysis is also covered. (Requirement: Prerequisite course or equivalent.)
90640.Title: Advanced Biochemistry

90641.details: Provides an introduction to manufacturing processes, traditional and nontraditional manufacturing processes and equipment; discusses interrelationships between material properties, the manufacturing process and the design of components; introduces how to perform manufacturing engineering evaluations and analyses. (Prerequisites: CHE 3260, AEE 3083, AEE 3083, MEE 3191, MEE 3191)
90641.Title: Manufacturing Processes

90642.details: Covers advanced topics reflecting the current research interests of the faculty and visiting scholars. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90642.Title: Sel Top Heat Trans/Energy
90642.Notes: TOPIC: Nanomaterials for Electrocatalytic Energy Conversion

90644.details: Provides a comprehensive understanding of the principles, design and applications of networks comprising small, battery-powered sensors that communicate wirelessly. Explores key topics such as sensor node architecture, communication protocols, energy-efficient algorithms, data aggregation and network deployment strategies. Equips students with the knowledge and skills to  develop and manage wireless sensor networks, which are essential for various applications, including environmental monitoring, industrial automation, health care and smart cities. Provides expertise in optimizing sensor network performance while conserving energy, making students valuable contributors to the rapidly evolving field of IoT and sensor-based technology.
90644.Title: Wireless Sensor Networks

90645.details: Provides a capstone  course that enables students to apply knowledge from all MSSE core courses to a complex system. Students are highly encouraged to engage with industries to work on real-life project settings or collaborate with the instructor to  identify a suitable complex system. Requires the submission of progress status throughout the course and the receipt of guidance from the instructor. Emphasizes practical application of systems engineering principles and  integration of learned concepts. (Prerequisites: SYS 5310, SYS 5315, SYS 5350, SYS 5365, SYS 5370, SYS 5460)
90645.Title: SE Design Project
90645.Notes: Course will run for the Fall-2 term (second 8-weeks)

90646.details: Discusses electrochemical energy systems, which play crucial roles in modern society, and electrochemical components, which  can be found everywhere, such as electric vehicles, computers, phones and grid-storage systems. Introduces the fundamental concepts and principles (e.g., thermodynamics, transport and kinetics) in electrochemical energy conversion and storage systems, such as electrolyzers, fuel cells, batteries and supercapacitors. (Prerequisites: CHE 3110, MEE 3191, MET 4501, PHY 3060)
90646.Title: Electrochemical Energy Sys

90647.details: Covers design and implementation of programs that communicate with other programs across a computer network. Includes common protocols, network management and debugging tools, server- and client-side networking, presentation layer, secure socket layer, multithreading, exceptions, remote procedure call and peer-to-peer networking. (Recommended: Background knowledge equivalent to CSE 2010.)
90647.Title: Network Programming

90648.details: Presents an understanding of historical and state of the art techniques in memory forensics. Explores forensics approaches for cybercrime and ransomware. Develops an understanding of current modular-designed memory forensics tools. Culminates in a research project to conduct and independent forensic investigation. (Prerequisites: CSE 3801)
90648.Title: Memory Forensics

90649.details: Covers theory and analysis of structures, aerodynamics, propulsion, space environment, standard atmosphere and control of aircraft and spacecraft. Presents the theoretical advances.  Introduces design, size and continuing developments from a historical perspective by  stressing the roles of weight estimation methods for and contributions of pioneers spacecraft. (Prerequisites: AEE 1202, AEE 1203, MTH 1002, MTH 1020, PHY 1001)
90649.Title: Aerospace Fundamentals 2

90650.details: Covers theory and analysis of structures, aerodynamics, propulsion, space environment, standard atmosphere and control of aircraft and spacecraft. Presents the theoretical advances.  Introduces design, size and continuing developments from a historical perspective by  stressing the roles of weight estimation methods for and contributions of pioneers spacecraft. (Prerequisites: AEE 1202, AEE 1203, MTH 1002, MTH 1020, PHY 1001)
90650.Title: Aerospace Fundamentals 2

Introduces design, size and continuing developments from a historical perspective by  stressing the roles of weight estimation methods for and contributions of pioneers spacecraft. (Prerequisites: AEE 1202, AEE 1203, MTH 1002, MTH 1020, PHY 1001)
90651.Title: Aerospace Fundamentals 2

90652.details: Examines the concepts and terminology of cyber operations from a practical point of view. Discusses ethical and legal considerations of cyber operations. Introduces vulnerability analysis and exploit development. Examines recent security-related trends  and technologies. Cross-listed with CSE 3801. Restricted to Computer Science, B.S. majors with Cyber Operations concentration. (Prerequisites: CSE 2010)
90652.Title: Intro to Cyber Operations

90653.details: Introduces formulation of precise definitions and their negations using propositional and predicate logic, argument analysis and proof techniques including induction, number theory, sets, relations, functions, directed graphs and elementary counting arguments. (Requirement(s): Passing score on placement test or prerequisite course) (Prerequisites: MTH 1000, MTH 1001, MTH 1010, MTH 1702)
90653.Title: Intro to Mathematical Proofs

90654.details: Selected topics from mathematics. Content varies from year to year depending on the needs and interests of the students and expertise of the instructor. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90654.Title: Spc Top in Applied Math

90655.details: A broad introduction to machine learning and data mining. Topics include supervised learning (feature selection, Bayesian classifier, nearest neighbors, logistic regression, decision trees, support vector machines) and unsupervised learning (dimensionality reduction, k-means clustering, hierarchical clustering, principal component analysis) methods. (Prerequisites: CSE 1001, CSE 1502, CSE 1503, MTH 2201, MTH 2202, MTH 3102, MTH 3200)
90655.Title: Intro to Machine Learning

90656.details: Principles of project management to design and develop products and services within budget, on time and to specification. Includes work planning, organization design, requirements analysis, project control and PERT/CPM.
90656.Title: Project Engineering
90656.Notes: For NGLU Engineering Mgmt students in Jan 2026 cohort

90657.details: Topics such as forecasting, plant location, facility layout, inventory systems, maintenance, process engineering, supply chains, scheduling, manufacturing and materials handling.

90657.Notes: For NGLU Engineering Mgmt students in Jan 2026 cohort

90658.details: Introduces engineering design optimization methods for decision-making in product design and manufacturing. Formulates and solves single-objective, multi-objective design problems. Formulates surrogate model-based design problems. Introduces decision-based design and robust design. Uses utility theory and game theory as design tools. (Requirement: Senior standing) (Prerequisites: MTH 2201, MTH 2202, MTH 3102, MTH 3200)

90659.details: Studies basic topology, continuous and semicontinuous functions, metric spaces, differentiation, measures, product measure, Lebesgue integration, Radon-Nikodym Theorem, Lp-spaces and measures on topological spaces. (Prerequisites: MTH 5110)

90660.details: Introduces students to practical methods and techniques for using artificial intelligence (AI) tools such as large language models (LLMs) and AI image generators using hands-on methods. Offers instruction on how to craft effective prompts, generate high-quality outputs and integrate AI tools into academic, creative and professional workflows.
90660.Title: Using AI Effectively

90663.details: Provides the opportunity to perform research with the research advisor before admission to candidacy. Enables the development of important skills necessary for an engineer or scientist to perform initial research to generate preliminary data. May be repeated for a maximum of 12 credits. Considered a full-load course when registered for three credit hours. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90663.Title: Research

90673.details: Studies specific problems of space sciences. (Requirement: Department head approval.)
90673.Title: Spc Top in Space Sciences

90674.details: Selected topics from mathematics. Content varies from year to year depending on the needs and interests of the students and expertise of the instructor. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90674.Title: Spc Top in Applied Math

90675.details: Explores emerging and specialized areas within the field of computer engineering. Provides an adaptable  outline, allowing topics to be tailored by the instructor based on their current research interests and the latest industry developments. Probes into cutting-edge subject matter, making this course an excellent choice for  those eager to explore the forefront of computer engineering and gain insights into the instructor's expertise and ongoing investigations. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90675.Title: Spc Top in Comp Engr

90676.details: Working closely with a faculty member, the student probes a subject in greater depth than is normally possible in a regular class. Requires a comprehensive paper or an applied research project. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)

90678.details: A project consisting of original research that will result in a substantial written work about a significant issue in the humanities. Serves as the culmination of a humanities major's undergraduate program. (Requirement: Senior standing and department head approval.) (HU) (Q) (LA) (Prerequisites: HUM 3999)

90679.details: Examines the ethical issues posed by  AI technologies to individuals, organizations and society. Provides conceptual tools for ethical decision-making through an interactive study of ethical theories, principles and case studies. Develops students? capacity for ethical problem-solving through the application of ethical theories and principles to AI policies and use cases of AI technologies.
90679.Title: AI, Ethics and Society

90680.details: Describes basic treatment techniques involved in behavioral treatment of children with autism. Includes behavioral characteristics and etiology of autism, history of applied behavior analysis for exceptional learners, and applies behavioral technologies and behavior change systems during supervised practical training. (Requirement: Prerequisite course or instructor approval.) (SS) (Prerequisites: PSY 3421)
90680.Title: ABA & Exceptional Learners

90681.details: Individual projects under the direction of faculty member in the chemical engineering program. Projects include a literature review, project proposal, process design or research, and written and oral reports. (Requirement: Department head approval or senior standing.)

(Prerequisites: CHE 3260, AEE 3083, AEE 3083, MEE 3191, MEE 3191)

90684.details: Analyzes, synthesizes and evaluates the major federal and state laws that impinge on the modern work environment. Draws on new insights in the human resources management discipline to abstract, summarize and evaluate the impact of legislation and laws regulating the employee/employer relationship.

(Prerequisites: CSE 1001, MTH 2201, MTH 3200, MTH 3102)

90688.details: Training in scoring and interpretation of intellectual assessment instruments covered in PSY 5521.
90688.Title: Lab/Assess of Intelligence

90689.details: Variable registration for dissertation completion after satisfaction of minimum registration requirements. (Requirements: Accepted candidacy and approval by Office of Graduate Programs.)
90689.Title: Final Semester Dissertation

90690.details: Provides students with the theoretical knowledge and hands-on experience in the entire pipeline from ideation to development to commercialization of medical technology solutions. Classroom lectures are complemented by expert guest seminars and a semester-long project on which students work with clinical partners in developing a technological solution for a real-world clinical need. (Note: Students from disciplines other than BME may take the course with the instructor?s approval.) (Prerequisites: BME 3240, BME 3240)
90690.Title: Med-Tech Innovations

90691.details: Aims to provide students with the theoretical knowledge and hands-on experience in the entire pipeline from ideation to development to commercialization of medical technology solutions. Classroom lectures are complemented by expert guest seminars and a semester-long project in which students work with clinical partners in developing a technological solution for a real-world clinical need.

90692.details: Participation in a research project under the direction of a faculty member. (Requirement: Instructor approval.) (Q)

90693.details: Develops an advanced and in-depth coverage of cost accounting and managerial accounting. Includes decision-making under uncertainty and risk, sensitivity analysis, strategic profitability analysis, cost allocation and resources, quality, and investment decisions and management control.
90693.Title: Advanced Cost & Mgr Acct

90694.details: Extends the principles of finance to an international context. Emphasizes currency fluctuation, measuring and hedging exchange rate risk, comparative capital structure, multinational investment, international capital budgeting and taxes. (WPS) (Requirement: Senior standing) (Prerequisites: BUS 3401)
90694.Title: Global Financial Mgmt

90695.details: Examines advanced financial theories and practices within an international framework. Analyzes currency dynamics, exchange rate risk measurement  and hedging strategies in global markets. Explores comparative capital structures, multinational investment decisions and cross-border capital budgeting. Includes consideration of taxation, regulatory environments and strategic financial policy in multinational enterprises.
90695.Title: Global Financial Mgmt

90696.details: Acquaints students with the tools and strategies they need to be successful information consumers and creators throughout their lifetimes. Teaches skills to help students identify reliable information sources for any context, along with practicing critical evaluation and ethical incorporation of sources into their own work. (LA) (Prerequisites: COM 1101, COM 1101)
90696.Title: Navigating the Info Landscape

90701.details: Preparation and submission of a research thesis on a selected topic in aviation human factors under the direction of the graduate faculty. (Requirement: Instructor approval.)
90701.Title: Thesis

90705.details: Provides a variable-credit, faculty-supervised research course designed to support graduate students engaged in original or advanced research in data science and optimization and closely related fields. Offers structured academic credit for independent research conducted under the guidance of a faculty mentor, focusing on a customized research topic aligned with the student?s academic goals, thesis, dissertation objectives or broader scholarly development. Research topics may span the full spectrum of and its interdisciplinary applications including but not limited to statistical modeling, stochastic processes, optimization, machine learning, artificial intelligence, statistical signal processing, data science and decision analytics. Emphasizes the development of research skills, methodological rigor and scholarly communication, preparing students for thesis or dissertation research, peer-reviewed publication and professional research careers. (Required: instructor approval) May be repeated for credit two times.
90705.Title: Research in DS and Opt

90707.details: The first of two courses in college-level writing skills. Focuses on writing essays using various rhetorical modes: persuasion, description, comparison and analysis. Presents basic methods of library research, as well as the MLA documentation system. Students write one research paper and several essays. (Requirement: Passing grade on placement exam or prerequisite course.) (LA) (Prerequisites: COM 0100, COM 0110, WRI 0100, WRI 0110)
90707.Title: Composition and Rhetoric

90708.details: Participation in a research project under the direction of a faculty member. (Requirement: Instructor approval.) (Q)
90708.Title: Undergraduate Research

90710.details: Includes research under the guidance of a faculty member in a selected area of meteorology.
90710.Title: Meteorological Research

90712.details: Provides academic instruction for an instrument rating. Includes principles of instrument flight, air traffic control, IFR procedures, analyses of weather information, IFR planning, emergency procedures and pilot decisions. Requires a score of 70 percent or higher on the FAA course completion examination. (Requirement: Special enrollment through the College of Aeronautics.) (Prerequisites: AVF 1001, AVT 1111)
90712.Title: Aeronautics 2

90713.details: Presents basic aeronautical factors affecting aircraft design and performance. Major topics include atmospheric properties, lift, drag, thrust, aircraft performance, stability and control, high-speed aerodynamics, operating strength limitations, and aerodynamics of specific flying problems. (Prerequisites: AVS 2101, EDS 1021, PHY 1001)
90713.Title: Aerodynamics

90714.details: Presents operational and managerial aspects of general aviation. Emphasizes corporate aviation. Includes fixed base operations (FBO), flight training, corporate aviation, general aviation aircraft, business aircraft ownership and management methods, and regulations associated with general aviation operations such as 14 CFR Parts 91 and 135. (Requirement: Junior or third year standing.)
90714.Title: General Aviation Ops & Mgt

90715.details: Covers federal U.S. air carrier regulation. Includes functions and relationships between the various major divisions of a typical air carrier. Prepares the student to take the FAA written exam for aircraft dispatcher and the FAA practical exam to receive an FAA Aircraft Dispatcher Certificate. (Requirement: Instructor approval or prerequisite course.) (Prerequisites: AVT 2001, AVT 2201)
90715.Title: Airline Operations

90716.details: Includes analysis and application of FAA and ICAO standards for airport design. Emphasizes the airside components. Includes runways; protection areas, zones and surfaces; taxiways; terminals; aprons; pavements; and on-airport facilities. (Requirement: Junior or third year standing.) (Prerequisites: AVM 3201)
90716.Title: Airport Design

90717.details: Provides an understanding of advanced aircraft performance, systems integrations and crew management. (Requirement: Instructor approval or prerequisite course.) (Prerequisites: AVF 2001)
90717.Title: Adv Aircraft Operations

90718.details: Studies modern airports, including their roles, functions and status in the national air transportation system; sponsorship and management alternatives; management of airport development, operations and business matters; and discussion of current and emerging public airport issues. (Requirement: Senior or fourth year standing.) (Prerequisites: AVM 3202)
90718.Title: Airport Management

90719.details: Requires students to plan, conduct and report on aviation research or scholarly activity. Includes a research plan, progress reports, a final research report and an oral and graphic summary and defense. May be repeated for a maximum of six credits. (Requirement: Senior standing.) (Q)
90719.Title: Aviation Research

90720.details: Applies business management concepts and techniques to the decision-making and problem-solving processes and situations in an aviation business. Uses operations research techniques, process analysis, forecasting, and computer and mathematical modeling as tools. (Q) (Prerequisites: AVM 4501)
90720.Title: Avt Business Simulation

90721.details: The first of two courses in college-level writing skills. Focuses on writing essays using various rhetorical modes: persuasion, description, comparison and analysis. Presents basic methods of library research, as well as the MLA documentation system. Students write one research paper and several essays. (Requirement: Passing grade on placement exam or prerequisite course.) (LA) (Prerequisites: COM 0100, COM 0110, WRI 0100, WRI 0110)

90722.Title: Writing About Literature

90723.details: Covers stress and strain; mechanical properties of materials; Hooke's law; axial, torsion, pure-bending and transverse loading of members; transformations of stress and strain; failure criteria; strain measurements; thin-walled pressure vessels; design for strength; energy methods; design for impact; and column buckling and stability. (Prerequisites: MAE 2081, MEE 2081)
90723.Title: Mech of Materials

90724.details: Provides a working familiarity with the physical principles, measurement and flow visualization techniques in fluid mechanics. (Prerequisites: AEE 3161, AEE 3161, PHY 2092)
90724.Title: Fluid Mechanics Laboratory

90725.details: Provides a working familiarity with the physical principles, measurement and flow visualization techniques in fluid mechanics. (Prerequisites: AEE 3161, AEE 3161, PHY 2092)
90725.Title: Fluid Mechanics Laboratory
